rationale: In the literature review, this citation is used to define a central empirical disagreement about how ad position affects conversion, not just as a background list item. The citing paper explicitly presents Agarwal, Hosanagar, and Smith (2011) as finding higher conversion at lower positions, then reports opposite evidence in its own data that higher positions improve conversion. The paper returns to this contrast in its discussion/conclusion, using it to position and interpret its contribution.
relation_description: The citing paper treats the cited study as a key conflicting benchmark in the position-conversion debate. It presents evidence that higher positions increase conversion, thereby contradicting the cited paper's lower-position conversion result.
